entry.title: Re: Looking to Understand User Exits in SAP SD with Real-...
entry.content:
Hi, Bikku.

UserExits, BADI's, and Enhancements are used to perform improvements and customizations in SAP standard programs.

When working with SAP systems, it is crucial not to modify the original standard code directly. This practice ensures that the system remains eligible for official SAP support and that the application of updates, patches, and system upgrades is not compromised. Modifying the standard code may result in the loss of warranty, difficulties in applying SAP Notes, and potential instability within the system.

The difference between them:

  • User-Exits:
    Predefined includes within SAP’s standard code where developers can insert custom programming logic. They may be User-modules (PBO/PAI) or User-forms.
    How to find User-Exits in SD:

    • Transaction: SE80 (Object Navigator)

    • Development Object section

    • Search for "VMOD" (The object VMOD contains all SD-related User-Exits).

  • Implicit Enhancements:
    A technique that allows customizations to standard SAP functionality without altering the core source code. Developers can inject their logic at predefined enhancement points.
    Example of creating an Implicit Enhancement:

    • Access SE38 -> Open program MV45AFZZ (commonly used for User-Exit modifications).

    • Click on Enhance (Spiral icon or SHIFT + F4).

    • Right-click within the editor -> Enhancement Operations -> Show Implicit Enhancement Options.

    • Choose an available point, right-click -> Enhancement Operations -> Create Enhancement.

    • Follow the guided process and add your custom logic.

    You can visualize existing Enhancements in a program using SE80.

  • BADI (Business Add-In):
    A more structured enhancement technique that uses interfaces and classes to insert custom business logic without modifying the SAP standard.

    • To search for BADI's: Use Transaction SE18.

    • To implement BADI's: Use Transaction SE19.

How to find BADI's in the system:

  • Classic BADI's:

    • Activate debug mode with /h during the process where you expect a BADI to be triggered.

    • Set a breakpoint (F9) in Method -> Global Class -> Class Name: CL_EXITHANDLER -> Method Name: GET_INSTANCE.

    • After executing (F8), check the EXIT_NAME variable under CHANGING to identify the BADI.

  • New BADI's:

    • Again, activate debug mode /h.

    • Set a breakpoint (F9) -> ABAP Commands -> CALL BADI.

    • Execute (F8) and inspect the TYPE REF of the variable being called to determine the relevant BADI.

Practical Example of a User-Exit Implementation(SD):
During my training, I developed a simple validation in transaction VA01 where, if the user attempted to save an order with more than 40 items, an error message would be displayed, preventing the save operation.
The implementation was done in the FORM USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE of the MV45AFZZ program.
Although I no longer have the code due to the passage of time, this project was crucial in helping me gain experience with debugging and the development of SAP enhancements.

Additional Note:

The YouTube channel "Mundo ABAP" offers great content related to ABAP, especially standard enhancements, with theoretical explanations and real-world examples. While the content is in Portuguese (PT-BR), YouTube’s automatic subtitle translation is generally sufficient to follow along and practice.

Understanding the various methods of enhancing SAP standard functionalities is crucial for any SAP developer or consultant. The information presented here serves only as an initial introduction and contextualization.
For a deeper understanding, it is highly recommended to explore additional resources such as blog articles, tutorial videos, and discussions available on SAP Community and other online learning sources.

I sincerely wish you all the best in your SAP journey, Hugo Loiola!

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Error importing project...
entry.content:
The reason why you are running into this issue is as follows...  Your application has dependencies.  So, let's assume the following.  
  • Your application
    • Dependency 1
    • Dependency 2
 
When you export your application, the exported file (mtar) contains everything (Your application + Dependency 1 + Dependency 2).  
 
But what you have probably done is...
  • Exported your application
  • Exported Dependency 1
  • Exported Dependency 2
  • Imported Dependency 1 (this works)
  • Imported Dependency 2 (this works)
  • Imported your application (this does not work - because this already includes Dependency 1 and 2)
 
Fix:
  • Delete Dependency 1 (if it exists in the Lobby already)
  • Delete Dependency 2 (if it exists in the Lobby already)
  • Import your application (this will import all 3 - Your application, Dependency 1, Dependency 2)

entry.title: How to enhance cats to work with PP orders
entry.content:
I have a customer that only wants to use CAT2 for all time entry.  The primary cost objects are WBS or Production Orders.  I understand CAT7 will not transfer PP order entries to controlling and an enhancement is necessary.  My question is what is the best approach? 

  • All time entries are auto approved and load to the CATSDB table in status 30.
  • WBS entries immediately create a CATSCO entry.
  • However, PP order entries do not create a CATSCO entry.
  • Is an enhance that creates the CATSCO entry the correct approach, and would CAT7 transfer to Controlling?
  • Is it better to use the BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_TT Function Module and pass the CATSDB data via an enhancement of CATA?
  • The system is S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2023 Initial FPS.

I'm trying to point my developer in the right direction.  Incidentally, I've working in customer system that only use CAT2 and not CO11n or CO12 for PP Order entry.  However, I'm not sure how they did it and there is no way to query.

entry.title: Re: Edit journal entry when processor approval Verify Gen...
entry.content:
Hello @evelu_dynasys ,

Please find the app document: Verify General Journal Entries | SAP Help Portal

  • For requestor, you can create, display, copy, and edit a journal entry, and then submit it for verification. 
  • For processer, you can choose to approve, reject, or suspend a journal entry, and then the system changes the status accordingly in the requester’s worklist.

Therefore, for the processer cannot edit the journal entry directly before posting. If the edit before posting is required, please try the following way:

  1. For requestor, withdraw a journal entry for editing before it's approved and resubmit it
  2. Processor rejects the journal entry, Journal Entry status is "Rejected" and the requester has to edit  and submit it again.

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: How to disable synchronization button for online cata...
entry.content:
HI @pavan-yvs-1 

Not sure if you're aware, but you can disable synchronization through permission settings in the Backoffice. 

So if you remove or revoke the synchronization permission from the users or user group, the synchronization button would not be available for them. 

To revoke or remove the permission, you can cross out or even drop the Synchronization permission entry.

And BTW, to check for online catalog versions, you can check the active property of the catalog version model, the version property might be changed to something else other than "Online".

catalogVersion.getActive()

Hope that helps 🙂

entry.title: Deeplink for "Display Credit Exposure" not opening the right page after authentication
entry.content:
We are providing a deeplink to "Display Credit Exposure" page in one of our custom Application. The deeplink is like below:

https://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/ui2/flp?sap-client=123&sap-language=EN#BusinessPartner-displayCreditExposure?BusinessPartner=12345

https://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/ui2/flp?sap-client=123&sap-language=EN#BusinessPartner-displayCreditExposure?BusinessPartner=12345

The link works fine if the user is already logged in. But if the user is not already logged in to SAP, it will ask for authentication, but after the authentication, it is getting redirected to the Home page. Not the "Display Credit Exposure" page. What I understood is this is because the parameters after the hash is not been sent to the server for authentication, hence it is missing this bit after the authentication.  

How can we overcome this issue ?  Is there anything wrong in my deeplink ? or is there something which we can add to the URL to force the redirect ?

 

Any input on this is appreciated.

 

Cheers

AJ

entry.title: Automatic Payment Reconciliation Using Japan Invoice Summary
entry.content:
Dear expert,

I’m raising this to seek further clarification regarding the automatic payment reconciliation functionality regarding the Japan Invoice Summary.

Based on my understanding, there are two approaches available to perform automatic payment allocation with Japan Invoice Summary:

(i)  Using Virtual Account (e.g., Bank Country Code, Bank Code, Virtual Account Number)

(ii) Using Alternative Name, Counterparty Bank Name, and Counterparty Bank Branch Name

I have configured option (ii) in the customer master data and successfully tested automatic payment reconciliation upon uploading the ZENGINKYO bank statement.

However, I would like to clarify the following points regarding option (ii):

  • During testing, I realized that the Alternative Name must exactly match the name maintained in the customer master for the reconciliation to proceed successfully. In practice, the ZENGINKYO bank statement may include additional details such as invoice numbers or internal notes appended to the payer name.
    → Therefore, may I further confirm if there is any tolerance or flexibility in matching the Alternative Name field? Or is a 100% exact match required?

Additionally:

  • When using the standard automatic payment reconciliation with Japan Invoice Summary (option ii),
    → Is it still necessary to maintain processing rules in the “Manage Processing Rules – For Bank Statements” app?
    In our test scenario, once I had configured a processing rule, the standard automatic payment reconciliation failed. I appreciate your confirmation on whether these rules are required or if they interfere with the standard Japan Invoice Summary logic.

Please also find the blog link(s) below for reference on the setup and behavior of automatic payment reconciliation using Japan Invoice Summary.

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/%E7%B7%A0%E3%82%81%E8%AB%8B%E6%B1%82%E3%82%92%E4%BD%BF%E3%81%A3%E3%81%9F%E5%85%A5%E9%87%91%E8%87%AA%E5%8B%95%E6%B6%88%E8%BE%BC%E3%81%AB%E3%81%A4%E3%81%84%E3%81%A6%E3%81%AE%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A8%E3%82%81/ba-p/13574735

Thanks in advance !

Regards,

Hong Yik

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance FIN (Finance)

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #5) Joule in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

 

In this blog, we will dive deep into Joule's integration with SAP SuccessFactors.

 

Let’s first quickly recap what we learnt so far:

  • Joule is an advanced, generative AI copilot.
  • It is embedded into various SAP applications, for example SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors etc.
  • Users ask their queries in the natively integrated Joule client embedded in SAP cloud solutions, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors etc. 
  • Joule analyses the prompt, provides a much richer query to the LLM, and finally returns a secure, accurate, and appropriate response back to the user.
  • Technically, Joule is a service running on SAP BTP in a Cloud Foundry environment.
  • Users can access Joule through a diamond icon in the top right-hand corner of the SAP user interface, regardless of the specific system. 
  • Joule uses SAP Cloud Identity Services for authentication and authorization.
  • Joule supports 4 interaction patterns - Informational, Navigational, Transactional, Analytical

 

Below image summarizes Joule's integration with SuccessFactors.

 

Joule in SuccessFactors

By integrating Joule with SAP SuccessFactors, organizations can leverage it’s capabilities for various HR tasks. For example, Joule can help customers get AI-driven insights, automate HR tasks, and provide instant access to key information.

Here are some of the questions you can ask Joule in SuccessFactors.

🔹How many vacation days do I have left?

🔹Show my performance goals for this quarter.

🔹What are the learning courses recommended for my role?

🔹Show me top-rated employees in my team.

 

How to Integrate Joule with SuccessFactors?

The SAP Discovery Centre mission Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors outlines all the steps required to set up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors.

Let’s have a quick look on these steps.

 

1. Check Prerequisites

SAP BTP Account with Joule

Verify Joule entitlements in your SAP BTP Global Account. If the Joule entitlement is missing, contact your SAP Account Executive.

 

 

SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition

Joule uses navigation service component of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to resolve intent based navigation targets and configure additional content providers.

Once customers sign up for Joule, they will be provided the Foundation Plan of SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition. Validate that the following 2 plans are available in SAP BTP global account.

 

SAP SuccessFactors License

Ensure you have a valid SAP SuccessFactors license and understand the Data Centers supported by Joule.

 

SAP Cloud Identity Services

Joule uses SAP Cloud Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning services to handle the provisioning of identities and their authorizations to SuccessFactors, SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule.

To set up a Joule instance that works across different SAP systems, they must be integrated with the same SAP Cloud Identity Service tenant.

 

Assign the required roles

To activate Joule with SuccessFactors, you need below roles.

  • SAP BTP Global Account Administrator and a BTP Subaccount Administrator
  • SAP SuccessFactors Administrator
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services Administrator

 

2. Register SuccessFactors in SAP BTP

Register your SAP SuccessFactors System in SAP BTP Global Account using SAP BTP System Landscape – formations.

 

 

3. Configure Subaccount in SAP BTP

To activate Joule, you need to configure a subaccount, enable cloud foundry and do few additional configurations. Establish the trust between IAS tenant that is configured for SuccessFactors with SAP BTP subaccount.

 

4. Run Booster

In SAP BTP, a "Booster" is a wizard-based, interactive setup guide. You need to run the booster “Setting up Joule”.

 

5. Post Booster Configurations

Finally, you need to do some configurations after booster is successfully executed.

 

Now you can open SuccessFactors and access Joule by clicking on diamond icon on top right corner.

This is in nutshell - Joule in SuccessFactors!

 

What's Next?

Blog #6 - Document Grounding in Joule [Will be published soon]

Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

entry.title: Calling an OData V4 custom action from within an iFlow.
entry.content:
Hi,

I've built a custom action in backend S4 using RAP. Its purpose is to sync a Z-table in S4 with data read from an on-prem SQL Server. It works perfectly from Postman when I call it via a POST to https://xxxHostnamexxxx:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_i_cv_person_api_o4/srvd_a2x/sap/zui_i_cv_person/0001/people/SAP__self.upsert_many .

I've tried incorporating it into an iFlow using the OData adaptor. This works fine for standard operations, like say using GET to return the top 10 records for the people entity. This is with Address: http://xxxCloudConnectorVirtualhostxxxx:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_i_cv_person_api_o4/srvd_a2x/sap/zui_i_cv_person/0001 and Resource Path: people.

From my Postman trial I know the URL to the custom action ends in people/SAP__self.upsert_many. I tried using this as the Resource Path, but I get the error "com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception. HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Resource path not found people/SAP__self.upsert_many". Is it possible to invoke a custom action using the OData adaptor?

I then tried using the HTTP adaptor to do a POST to the explicit URL, but I have to manage the CSRF protection manually and I can't work out how to pass the CSRF token to the POST operation after having called a GET to obtain the token. In this case I get this error {"error":{"code":"/IWBEP/CM_V4H_RUN/043","message":"CSRF token is missing","@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory":"CSRF_Token_Missing"}}

This is my first time using ABAP RAP and first time with SAP BTP. Maybe passing data en masse via a custom action isn't the right approach. Any help would be appreciated. 

- Anthony.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story Time dimension issue (Previous year data di...
entry.content:
1. Define a page filter(input control) or a story filter with range on time dimension 
  2. Create a current date input control with month granularity
  3. Define a range with lookback with 30 years

User select the month from current date input control, report shows all the period back 30 years.

entry.title: Re: SAC Modeler - load data into a model - defines blank...
entry.content:
It's a current limitation. One workaround: split data load into two jobs: 

  1. Job 1 with data having parent member value for that hierarchy, map the column to hierarchy property 
  2. Job 2 with data having parent member empty, DON"T map hierarchy property in this case.

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/enhance-mash-up-screen-for-custom-fields/ba-p/13555918
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002448908 

It is needed to consider that mashups uses new infotype framework which can be tested at puit_ui, you can use as reference to be sure the new IT framework is capable of using that custom IT. 

If mashup is used for viewing only then it can replicate this data but if it is used for maintenance of the data this data should not be replicated. Other case the IT will be mantained in 2 different ways and data from the mashup will be overwritten by replication. It needs to be chosen which maintenance method you prefer if mashup or replication. 

Hope this helps, 
Regards,

entry.title: How to Build AI chatbot using SAPUI5 , CAP service and AI Core API?
entry.content:
Dear all,
I implemented this AI chatbot functionality in SAPUI5 apps with SAP CAP backend and AI core API.
I thought of sharing my knowledge with you all. I took references from multiple blogs to complete this functionality, I will list all those blogs in the references section below and thank you for all the authors of those blogs.
Steps involved to implement this functionality,

  1. Create a destination in SAP BTP pointing your AI API.
  2. Configure this destination in SAP CAP backend service and UI level.
  3. Define action in the CAP service and call the destination to read data from SAP AI API.
  4. Use this action from CAP service in UI5 app and integrate CAP service API and UI5 app.

 

  1. Create a destination in SAP BTP pointing your AI API:

Name: URL: <AI_API_URL>/v2 # Eg. https://api.ai....ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2 <- Note the suffix /v2 is very important here

Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials

Client ID: <CLIENT_ID> -> you can get it from SAP AI core instance service key

Client Secret: <CLIENT_SECRET>  ->  you can get it from AI core instance service key

Token Service Url: <TOKEN_URL>/oauth/token   -> append this to Token URL

## Additional Properties

HTML5.DynamicDestination: true

URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group: default -> Just add key and value in properties even you do not have drop down values.

URL.headers.Content-Type: application/json -> Just add key and value in properties even you do not have drop down values. 

 

 

 

2. Configure this destination in SAP CAP backend service and UI level:

            Backend Cap service -> package.json

 "cds": {

    "requires": {

      "AI_ASSISTANT": {

        "kind": "rest",

        "[hybrid]": {

          "credentials": {

            "destination": "AI_ASSISTANT", -> Destination name here

            "path": "/inference/deployments/<deployment ID here>"

          }

        },

        "[production]": {

          "credentials": {

            "destination": "AI_ASSISTANT",

            "path": "/inference/deployments/<deployment ID here>"

          }

        }

      }

    },

  }

Note: You can get Deployment Id from AI launchpad.

UI5 app level- > xs-app.json

Add a new route,

{
      "source": "^/api/(.*)$",
      "target": "/inference/deployments/<deployment ID here>/$1",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "destination": "AI_ASSISTANT"
    }

 3. Define action in the CAP service and call the destination to read data from SAP AI API:

   We will define action in CAP backend service to call the AI API through destination.

   db/schema.cds:

type AIChatBotResponse {
        text : String;
    }

       srv/service.cds:

service DemoService {
 action getAIResponse(query : String) returns AIChatBotResponse;
}

srv/service.js file: 

 this.on("getAIResponse", async (request: Request) => {
            const { query } = request.data;
            const aiDestination = await cds.connect.to("AI_ASSISTANT");
            const aiChatBotResponse = await aiDestination.send('POST','/v2/predict', { query: query })
            //return aiChatBotResponse;
            return { text: aiChatBotResponse.response };

        })

 Note: import above defined type from schema.cds into service definition cds file.

4. Use this action from CAP service in UI5 app and integrate CAP service API and UI5 app:

For UI part you can take dialog and display chat dialog on click of a AI chat bot button.

XML View/fragment code:

<core:FragmentDefinition
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    xmlns:form="sap.ui.layout.form"
    xmlns:u="sap.ui.unified"
    xmlns:layout="sap.ui.layout"
>
  <Dialog
        showHeader="false"
        verticalScrolling="false"
        contentHeight="60%"
        contentWidth="40%"
        draggable="true"
        resizable="true"
    >
<Page >
    <VBox width="100%" height="auto">
        <IllustratedMessage id="id_illuMsg" 
                                title="{i18n>aiChatBotTitle}" 
                                class="sapUiLargeMarginTop"
                                description="{i18n>aiChatbotAskMe}" 
                                illustrationType="tnt-FaceID"
                                illustrationSize="Auto">
            <additionalContent>
                <Button text="{i18n>aiChatbotBtnText}" press="onPressHide"/>
            </additionalContent>
            </IllustratedMessage>
           


        <ScrollContainer id="id_SC" visible="false" 
                        width="100%"
                        height="auto" 
                        vertical="true">
        <List id="id_List" items="{path:'chatbot>/list'}"
                            noDataText="{i18n>aiChatBotNoChat}">    
            <items>
            <FeedListItem text="{chatbot>text}" 
                    icon="{= ${chatbot>type} === 'query' ?                                        
                    'sap-icon://customer' : 'sap-icon://da-2' }">
            </FeedListItem>                        
            </items>
        </List>
        </ScrollContainer>
        <VBox id="id_box" visible="false" width="100%">
        <Input width="100%" 
                placeholder="{i18n>AiChatbotInputPlaceholder}"
                submit="onSubmitQuery"/>
        </VBox>
    </VBox>
</Page>
        <endButton>
            <Button
                text="{i18n>close}"
                type="Default"
                press="onAIChatbotClose"
            />
        </endButton>
   </Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Component.js:
This is for adding Illustration types in chatbot dilaog. We should register them in the Illustration pool.
https://ui5.sap.com/#/api/sap.m.IllustratedMessage 

init: async function () {
                // call the base component's init function
                UIComponent.prototype.init.apply(this, arguments);

                // enable routing
                this.getRouter().initialize();

                // set the device model
                this.setModel(models.createDeviceModel(), "device");
              

                // code for AI chatBot to register illustartions/icons in the chat dialog
                var oTntSet = {
                    setFamily: "tnt",
                    setURI: sap.ui.require.toUrl("sap/tnt/themes/base/illustrations")
                };
    
                // register tnt illustration set
                IllustrationPool.registerIllustrationSet(oTntSet, false);
                
            },

 

 

UI code controller js/ts file:

onPressAIAssistant: function () {
            let oView = this.getView();
            if (!this._oAIDialog) {
                this._oAIDialog = Fragment.load({
                    id: oView.getId(),
                    name: "yournamesapce.fragment.AIChatbot",
                    controller: this
                }).then(function (oDialog) {
                    oView.addDependent(oDialog);
                    return oDialog;
                }.bind(this));
            }
            this._oAIDialog.then(function (oDialog) {
               
                oDialog.open();
            }.bind(this));
        },
        onPressHide: function(oEvent) {
            let oView = this.getView();
            let oBtn = oEvent.getSource();
            oView.byId("id_SC").setVisible(true);
            oView.byId("id_box").setVisible(true);
            oBtn.setVisible(false);
        },

 

onSubmitQuery: function (oEvent) {
            let oInput = oEvent.getSource();
            let sQuery = oInput.getValue();
            oInput.setValue("");
    
            // Add User query to the model
            this.aList =  this.ChatbotJSONModel.getProperty("/list");
            if (!this.aList) {
              this.aList = [];
            }
    
            let oJsonObj = {
              text: sQuery,
              type: "query",
            };
            this.aList.push(oJsonObj);
            this.ChatbotJSONModel.setProperty("/list", this.aList);
 
            this.aList = this.ChatbotJSONModel.getProperty("/list");

            let oPayLoad = {};
            oPayLoad.query = sQuery
            let oDataModel = this.getView()?.getModel();
             const that = this;
                return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                    oDataModel.callFunction("/getAIResponse", {
                        method: "POST",
                        urlParameters: oPayLoad,
                        success: function (oDataReceived) {
                            if (oDataReceived) {
                                if (oDataReceived && oDataReceived.getAIResponse) {
                                    let aiResponse = oDataReceived.getAIResponse;
                                    let oJsonObj = {
                                        text: aiResponse.text,
                                        type: "response",
                                      };
                                    that.aList.push(oJsonObj);
                                    that.ChatbotJSONModel.setProperty("/list", that.aList);
                                    resolve(aiResponse);
                                    }
                            }
                        },
                        error: function (oErrorReceived) {
                           reject(oErrorReceived);
                        }
                    });
                });

          }

UI screenshot:

References:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/a-chatgpt-like-chat-app-built-with-modern-sap-technologies-cap-sapui5-part/ba-p/13549057

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/integrating-ai-with-sapui5-fiori-apps-part-1-concept/ba-p/13734579

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/custom-chatbot-widget-in-sapui5-using-expression-binding/ba-p/13867135

https://github.com/SAP-samples/azure-openai-aicore-cap-api/blob/main/documentation/02-cap-api/01-prepare-cap-deployment.md

Best Regards,
Venkatesh.

entry.title: Re: Why my deploy "Deploy using cf deploy" is failed?
entry.content:
When the "bookstore-db" is in status failed it means it couldn't create the HDI container (schema) in your HANA database. So best would be to verify if your HANA Cloud is created, running, and mapped to the subaccount/space you are deploying your application in. (Step 1) You can also click on the "failed" message in the cockpit to learn more about the reason.

Once verified that HC is ready, you have to delete "bookstore-db" via the cockpit and retry the deployment.

entry.title: Standard Web Service for ATP Check and Release of Customer Demand in Outbound Logistics
entry.content:
In SAP Business ByDesign, is there any standard web service to check ATP of customer demand, and to release customer demand?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Standard Web Service for ATP Check and Release of Cus...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

There is a standard web service CustomerRequirementATPCheck, that can be used to simulate ATP check. It returns the result of the ATP check, but it won't impact the ATP status of any customer demand.

There is no standard web service to release customer demand.

Henry

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Utilities Roadmap 2025 Onwards
entry.content:
Hello @Amit200385,

I don´t expect any updates regarding the templates in future releases, because the MDT templates are already available for years. The effort for setting up these templates lies especially in determining the attribute values. For your case the question is if these ones are given by the calling application or if these ones have to be determined in SAP. I would expect that the calling application calls the Odata with the expected values. 
Of course objects can also be created without MDT templates, but then you will need to do several calls and so a sequence needs to be set up. Personally I find MDT templates a good way to create objects, but sometimes other ways are better. It depends on your use case. Also open for a chat about your use case.

Regards,

Pieter

entry.title: Re: SAP PS
entry.content:
Hi Ali,

In SAP PS, when you do the TECO, it signals that the work is finished, however the system still allow certain changes i.e. confirmations, dates, do services entry sheets even after TECO. But, you can't  do major planning activities, like creating new network/activity. To prevent the changes after WBS TECO, you can specify user status, linked with WBS TECO status in control you forbidden changes like CN22, CJ20N editing. 

I hope this help with your query. Let me know if you need any further assistance.

entry.title: BYD2502 Cannot change implementation because of status Tested
entry.content:
Implementation project is in status Tested so that it is impossible to continue with changes. How can we set another status?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2502 Cannot change implementation because of statu...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

To revert the Project status from Tested to Started, please follow the below steps:

  1. Go to Business Configuration work center.
  2. Select Implementation Projects view.
  3. Highlight Implementation Project and select Open Activity List button.
  4. Go to Test phase.
  5. Highlight Confirm Milestone: Go-Live Readiness Acceptance activity and select Open button.
  6. Uncheck Go-Live Readiness Accepted check box.
  7. Select Confirm button.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Technical Support,

Cariford

entry.title: Custom Personas vs. Multiple Custom Instances in SSAM
entry.content:
Hi all,

We currently have a requirement to have multiple SAP Service Asset Manager (SSAM) mobile applications . However, we are facing challenges in managing a common configuration across all instances.

To address this, I am exploring alternative approaches that would allow us to implement independent configurations for each application. So far, we have identified two possible options:
1. Custom Personas
2. Multiple Custom Instances

While researching, I came across a blog post where SAP clearly states that using multiple custom instances is  recommended approach for productive systems.

Given this, I’d like to ask if anyone has experience working with custom personas or custom instances in a similar context. Your insights into any challenges or limitations you’ve faced would be extremely valuable in helping us determine the most stable and scalable solution.

Additionally, if there are any blogs, documentation, or best practices available around creating and managing custom personas, please feel free to share those as well.

Looking forward to your thoughts and suggestions.

entry.title: SAP Signavio April 2025 Product Release - Tech Partnerships
entry.content:
In today's fast-evolving business landscape, partnerships are key to delivering faster, smarter, and value-driven transformations for our customers. With the SAP Signavio April 2025 Product Release, we are excited to share a few new technology partnerships that further expand the value of our offering. By partnering with Konekti, Medallia, and Mimica, we are strengthening our ecosystem further to drive even greater process insights, operational efficiency, and customer-centric innovation.

At SAP Signavio, we believe we are better together — combining strengths across a trusted ecosystem to help organizations break down silos, maximize the value of their data, and unlock transformational outcomes at scale.

 

Konekti

Trusted by top practitioners, Konekti is redefining process intelligence by tackling the biggest bottleneck—data transformation. Built by experts from the birthplace of process mining, the Konekti platform accelerates insights, reduces costs, and integrates seamlessly with existing tech stacks.

SAP Signavio's partnership with Konekti enhances our portfolio by accelerating object-based data modeling , while reducing process mining migration efforts and offering transformative efficiency in large-scale projects. It empowers your process intelligence initiatives with high-quality and trusted data models, integrated with SAP Signavio. Generate actionable insights faster, increase the ROI of process intelligence, and deliver trusted insights.

For more information see here.

 

Medallia

Medallia is a market leader in customer, employee, citizen, and patient experience. As one of the top enterprise experience platforms, Medallia Experience Cloud is the experience system of record that makes all other applications customer and employee aware.

Medallia complements our SAP Signavio portfolio by enriching process data with customer insights. It collect structured and unstructured customer and employee signals at scale, complement it with operational and observational data, analyze it, and distribute actionable insights to every level of the organization in real-time to streamline SAP Signavio processes for your clients and break down organizational silos.

For more information see here.

 

Mimica

Mimica is an AI-powered task mining company that automates repetitive desktop tasks and enhance productivity. By delivering rapid, data-driven recommendations, Mimica helps organizations reclaim time, reduce costs, and transform operations at scale. The company has saved clients over 1 million hours of productivity and is trusted by some of the world’s largest enterprises.

Mimica aligns with our open ecosystem strategy and extends the options to gather and analyze task-level data to complement our process mining capabilities. With AI-powered task mining observes employee actions across the desktop and categorizes them into detailed process maps. This process intelligence highlights inefficiencies, prioritizes improvements by ROI, and provides blueprints for automation, including GenAI, RPA, and IDP. With this, teams can maximize workforce productivity, cut costs, and drive operational excellence across your ECC or S4HANA ecosystems.

For more information see here.

entry.title: SAP Signavio April 2025 Product Release - Process Governance and Collaboration
entry.content:
SAP Signavio April 2024 Release

Process Governance and Collaboration


SAP Signavio Process Governance

Within SAP Signavio Process Governance, we are releasing the following highlight:

Maintain Compliance with Configurable Case Retention

With the new configurable case retention feature, administrators can now easily define who can manage retention settings and automate the deletion of closed cases after a defined period. This feature enables organizations to ensure compliance by automatically removing outdated cases in line with regulatory policies, simplify governance through clearly assigned permissions, and optimize system performance by reducing data clutter. It also strengthens data governance by enforcing strict approval processes, ensuring that only authorized users can modify or export retention-related information. 

 

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub continues to evolve as a central space where teams align, share, and drive transformation. We're pleased to introduce a set of new enhancements, some of which span across the broader SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite, designed to simplify daily work, improve access to information, and strengthen collaboration across your organization.

 

New unified Dictionary UI in Collaboration Hub to enable consumption of Dictionary content

For those navigating the complexities of process modeling, the exciting new update to SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub is designed with you in mind. The newly unified dictionary user interface promises to enhance your experience by making the exploration of dictionary content easier and more intuitive than ever. With a comprehensive table view and detailed pages for dictionary entries, finding and utilizing crucial information becomes a breeze. This improved accessibility benefits both process experts and casual hub users alike, ensuring that everyone can quickly and easily access the content they need. Embrace this user-friendly update to streamline your workflows and empower your decision-making with readily available insights.

Click here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Re-generate reports with advanced filtering

Managing reports is now more efficient with updates that let you quickly access and reuse up to 50 of your most recently generated reports. Instead of starting from scratch, users can regenerate reports using saved filters, rename them for better organization, or download them for immediate use.

Advanced filtering capabilities also make it easier to find the exact content you need during report creation, helping reduce time spent searching and improving accuracy.

These improvements are especially valuable for process modelers and administrators who need to generate regular reports with minimal manual effort, while maintaining consistency across teams and use cases.

Click here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

Folder hierarchy view for access rights

Managing user and group access across your workspace is now simpler and more transparent with the new “folder hierarchy” view in SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub. This feature provides a full view of your folder structure and the associated permissions, helping administrators quickly identify who has access to what content for example "Shared Documents" in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub and "Repository" of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. With an intuitive interface for reviewing and adjusting access rights, administrators can resolve permission issues faster, maintain better control over sensitive content, and support compliance efforts more effectively. This update is a step toward a more unified and manageable permissions model across the entire SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite, giving you the clarity and control needed to confidently govern your workspace. 

Click here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

entry.title: how to make alv editable for any two fields& add save and delete buttons using factory method
entry.content:
REPORT zcls_factory1.

DATA: lt_data  TYPE STANDARD TABLE  OF zcds_ekko,
      lt_data1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF  zcds_vbap.


DATA:lo_alv  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.


SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
  PARAMETERS: p_r1 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad1 DEFAULT 'X' USER-COMMAND rad,
              p_r2 RADIOBUTTON GROUP rad1.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

DATA: l_ebeln TYPE ebeln,
      l_vbeln TYPE vbeln_va.
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b2 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-002.
  SELECT-OPTIONS: s_ebeln FOR l_ebeln MODIF ID po,
                  s_vbeln FOR l_vbeln MODIF ID so.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b2.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.


  IF p_r1 = 'X'.
    LOOP AT SCREEN.
      IF screen-group1 = 'SO'.
        screen-active = 0.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.

  IF p_r1 = ' '.
    LOOP AT SCREEN.
      IF screen-group1 = 'PO'.
        screen-active = 0.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.
*
*class class1 DEFINITION.
*  PUBLIC SECTION.
*  methods handler  for EVENT ADDED_FUNCTION of  cl_salv_events_table.
*  ENDCLASS.
*
*  class class1 IMPLEMENTATION.
*    METHOD handler.
*      ENDMETHOD.
*    ENDCLASS.




START-OF-SELECTION..


  IF p_r1 = 'X'.
    SELECT * FROM zcds_ekko INTO TABLE @LT_data  WHERE ebeln IN @s_ebeln.
  ELSE.

    SELECT * FROM zcds_vbap INTO TABLE @LT_data1 WHERE salesorder IN @s_vbeln.
  ENDIF.

*          creating alv report

*  SET PF-STATUS 'DISPLAY'.



DATA:lo_event TYPE REF TO cl_salv_events_table.


*       grv_alv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
*            DATA LIST_DISPLAY   TYPE SAP_BOOL.
*            DATA R_CONTAINER    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER.
*            DATA CONTAINER_NAME TYPE STRING.
*            DATA R_SALV_TABLE   TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_TABLE.
*            DATA T_TABLE        TYPE TABLE.
  IF p_r1 = 'X'.
*    refresh lo_alv.
    cl_salv_table=>factory(
*              EXPORTING
*                list_display   = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>FALSE
*                r_container    = r_container
*                container_name = container_name
      IMPORTING
        r_salv_table   = lo_alv
      CHANGING
        t_table        = lt_data
           ).
  ENDIF.

  IF  p_r2 = 'X'.

*    refresh lo_alv.
    cl_salv_table=>factory(
*           EXPORTING
          list_display   = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>FALSE
*           r_container    = r_container
*                container_name = container_name
    IMPORTING
      r_salv_table   = lo_alv
    CHANGING
      t_table        = lt_data1
         ).
  ENDIF.

  lo_alv->set_screen_status(
                     report        = sy-repid
                    pfstatus      = 'DISPLAY'
*                 set_functions = C_FUNCTIONS_NONE
 ).

**DATA VALUE TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE.
*
  lo_event = lo_alv->get_event( ).




" Set the event handler for the top of page event
*SET HANDLER top_of_page_handler FOR lo_event->top_of_page.

*
*  data: lo_obj type REF TO class1.
*
*  CREATE OBJECT lo_obj.
*  set HANDLER lo_obj->handler for lo_event.






  CALL METHOD lo_alv->display( ).

entry.title: Re: Express document "Update was terminated" for BAPI BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2
entry.content:
Hello RaymondGiuseppi,

I am facing simmilar issue with BAPI_PO_CHNAGE could you please suggest any solution for below issue?

We are using 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE' to modify Delivery date, some times after 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' we are getting error “Express document "Update was terminated" received from author”. If we run same file second time it will get processed successfully.

 

 

Can anyone please let me know how to suppress this error.

 

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      PURCHASEORDER                = T_EKET-EBELN
*     POHEADER                     = T_HEADER
*     POHEADERX                    = T_HEADERX
*     POADDRVENDOR                 =
*     TESTRUN                      =
*     MEMORY_UNCOMPLETE            =
*     MEMORY_COMPLETE              =
*     POEXPIMPHEADER               =
*     POEXPIMPHEADERX              =
*     VERSIONS                     =
*     NO_MESSAGING                 =
*     NO_MESSAGE_REQ               =
*     NO_AUTHORITY                 =
*     NO_PRICE_FROM_PO             =
*   IMPORTING
*     EXPHEADER                    =
*     EXPPOEXPIMPHEADER            =
   TABLES
     RETURN                       = RETURN
*     POITEM                       = T_POITEM
*     POITEMX                      = T_POITEMX
*     POADDRDELIVERY               = T_POADDRDELIVERY
     POSCHEDULE                   = T_POSCHEDULE
     POSCHEDULEX                  = T_POSCHEDULEX
*     POACCOUNT                    =
*     POACCOUNTPROFITSEGMENT       =
*     POACCOUNTX                   =
*     POCONDHEADER                 =
*     POCONDHEADERX                =
*     POCOND                       = T_POCOND
*     POCONDX                      =
*     POLIMITS                     =
*     POCONTRACTLIMITS             =
*     POSERVICES                   =
*     POSRVACCESSVALUES            =
*     POSERVICESTEXT               =
*     EXTENSIONIN                  =
*     EXTENSIONOUT                 = T_EXTENSIONOUT
*     POEXPIMPITEM                 =
*     POEXPIMPITEMX                =
*     POTEXTHEADER                 =
*     POTEXTITEM                   =
*     ALLVERSIONS                  =
*     POPARTNER                    = T_POPPARTNER
*     POCOMPONENTS                 =
*     POCOMPONENTSX                =
*     POSHIPPING                   =
*     POSHIPPINGX                  =
*     POSHIPPINGEXP                =
*     POHISTORY                    =
*     POHISTORY_TOTALS             =
*     POCONFIRMATION               =
            .
  READ TABLE RETURN  WITH KEY TYPE = 'E' .
  IF SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'
*     IMPORTING
*       RETURN        =
              .
    DELETE RETURN WHERE TYPE NE 'E'.
    LOOP AT RETURN.
      IT_ERROR-MESS = RETURN-MESSAGE.
      IT_ERROR-PO = T_EKET-EBELN.
      APPEND IT_ERROR.
    ENDLOOP.
  ELSE.
    READ TABLE RETURN  WITH KEY TYPE = 'A' .
    IF SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.             .
      DELETE RETURN WHERE TYPE NE 'A'.                                             
      LOOP AT RETURN.
        IT_ERROR-MESS = RETURN-MESSAGE.
        IT_ERROR-PO = T_EKET-EBELN.
        APPEND IT_ERROR.
      ENDLOOP.
    ELSE.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
      EXPORTING
        WAIT          = 'X'.
 
ENDIF.

entry.title: How to configure late Coming and Early Leaving in SuccessFactors TimeSheet/TimeTracking?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on Timesheet/Time-Tracking.

I have below scenarios, could anyone help me with the configuration please. We are following Clock based work schedules. We also have Time Tracking in place.

Late Coming Scenario:

  1. The Grace period is 15 mins.
  2. If the employee is coming after 15 minutes and up to 2 hours this will be considered as late coming, We should have a time valuation to capture these late comings.
  3. If the employee is coming after 2 hours up to 4 hours, this will be considered as half day absence. We should have a time valuation to capture these half day absences.
  4. If the employee is coming after 4 hours, this will be considered as full day absence. We should have a time valuation to capture these half day absences.

Early Leaving Scenario:

  1. Grace period is 5 mins.
  2. If the employee is leaving before 5 minutes and up to 2 hours this will be considered as early leaving, We should have a time valuation to capture these early leavings.
  3. If the employee is leaving before 2 hours and up to 4 hours this will be considered as half day absence, We should have a time valuation to capture these half day absences.
  4. If the employee is leaving before 4 hours this will be considered as full day absence, We should have a time valuation to capture these full day absences.

Based on the above valuations HR will deduct the leave balance manually every month. Here the month is from 20th to 19th. (Ex April 20th to May 19th).

Please suggest me how to configure the above requirement.

Regards

Hafeez

entry.title: Re: SAP DataServices setup automation
entry.content:
Hi @asdfsadfasdf 

 

  1. Configure DSN with SSL - Command line: Data Services Connection Manager (Unix)
    • the command below should show you the needed parameters
      • $LINK_DIR/bin/DSConnectionManager.sh -help
  2. Test Connection with CMC console -- More context needed on this, do you mean testing the connection to CMC?, Testing Repository connection in CMC? - Not clear
    • Command Line Administration
  3. Set up AccessServer - Command line: Adding an Access Server (Unix or Linux)
  4. Setup JobServer - Command line: Adding a Job Server (Unix or Linux)
  5. Define RT services and instances from DS Management console --> Currently not possible
  6. Configure Web Services from DS Management Console 
    • Using SAP Data Services as a SOAP Web Service provider
    • Using SAP Data Services as a RESTful Web Service Provider

BR,

Joe

entry.title: April 2025 Product Release Process and Journey Modeling
entry.content:
SAP Signavio solutions are designed to help companies thrive. By providing a clear view of your processes, SAP Signavio enables you to observe how your company is running, derive actionable insights, and transform those insights into continuous improvement loops. 

 

In this April 2025 release, we have introduced several powerful updates designed to simplify decision modeling, improve process lifecycle management, streamline report generation, and enhance journey modeling. Whether you're looking to optimize decision models, maintain data consistency, or create impactful customer journeys, these enhancements empower you to achieve more with greater efficiency and precision. 

 

Let's dive into what's new in this latest SAP Signavio release. 

 

SAP Signavio Process Manager  

Enhanced DMN TestLab UX for streamlined decision modeling 

For decision modelers, managing complex decision models and test cases can be a challenging task. But what if there was a way to simplify the process, enhance usability, and accelerate your decision modeling workflow? Empower decision modelers with a streamlined and intuitive DMN TestLab UX, designed to simplify test case management and accelerate decision modeling. With improved efficiency and enhanced usability, the platform offers intuitive tools, filtering, and grouping to simplify navigation through complex decision models. Additionally, the system supports scalable management, making it easier to handle growing decision model requirements. 

 

 Click here to watch the Enable Now video. 

 

Extended lifecycle control of synchronized process models between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM based on approval status 

In today’s fast-paced business environment, maintaining data consistency and control over your processes is crucial. Organizations using the integration of SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM can now: 

  • Enhance process control and data consistency with automated synchronization of approved process model revisions. 
    • Reduce manual effort for process managers by streamlining the process and implementation lifecycle with automatic updates based on approval status.  
      • Benefit from greater flexibility with configurable synchronization settings, ensuring only approved revisions are seamlessly transferred. 

        This feature not only reduces the complexity of managing process models but also empowers teams to focus on more strategic initiatives, knowing their process data is always up to date and aligned. 

          

         

        Report history and advanced filtering for quick report generation 

        Reporting is a crucial part of process management, but it’s often time-consuming and manual. That’s why we’re excited to introduce an enhancement to SAP Signavio Process Manager that makes report retrieval faster, easier, and more efficient than ever before. Now, process experts can quickly access up to 50 of their most recently generated reports—without having to rebuild them from scratch. This feature streamlines the reporting experience, significantly reducing the manual effort involved and enabling a more productive workflow. 

          

         

        Supporting collapsed subprocesses and intermediate link events when synchronizing process models to SAP Solution Manager 

        In the current dynamic corporate landscape, clarity and efficiency in process management are more important than ever. That's why synchronizing your tools can make a huge difference—especially when it comes to connecting SAP Signavio Process Manager with SAP Solution Manager. Enhance process transparency and navigation by synchronizing collapsed subprocesses and intermediate link events from SAP Signavio Process Manager to SAP Solution Manager as process links. Gain a comprehensive view of related processes with seamless synchronization of linked diagrams, ensuring better understanding of process relationships. Plus, improve usability with direct navigation between interconnected processes, enabling a more efficient and intuitive workflow. 

         

         Click here to watch the Enable Now video. 

         

        Supporting mapping of activities from dictionary subcategories when synchronizing to SAP Solution Manager 

        As organizations continue to refine their process landscapes, the ability to customize and fine-tune synchronization becomes increasingly valuable. With enhanced capabilities for mapping dictionary activities, users can now take greater control over their synchronization projects—driving both flexibility and consistency. Improve synchronization accuracy by incorporating activities from subcategories or main categories, ensuring a more comprehensive mapping process. Deliver a more intuitive user experience with a connector that reflects diverse dictionary structures. Additionally, simplify migration from the classic connector by replicating familiar functionality for a seamless transition. 

        Click here to watch the Enable Now video. 

         

        Identification of synchronized processes as template-based, variant, or standalone when synchronizing with SAP Solution 

        Effective process management starts with clarity—and in complex enterprise landscapes, that means knowing exactly what type of process you're working with. Enhance process management in SAP Solution Manager by clearly marking synchronized process types—variant, template, or standalone—ensuring users can accurately identify and categorize processes. This clarity enables more efficient system configuration, testing, and documentation, allowing users to tailor and customize processes with confidence. By streamlining process identification, organizations can optimize synchronization and maintain consistency across their workflows. 

         Click here to watch the Enable Now video. 

         

        SAP Signavio Journey Modeler 

        Quick start journey modeling with best-practice journey models and journey model templates 

        Creating seamless, impactful customer journeys can be a challenging task—but it doesn’t have to be. With the Value Accelerator Library for SAP Signavio, journey modelers now have access to best-practice journey models and templates that can be easily personalized to suit your unique needs. Designed to help you optimize customer experiences and accelerate the journey modeling process, this library provides the tools you need to fast-track model creation, ensuring that you can create meaningful, effective journeys in less time. 

        Click here to watch the Enable Now video. 

         

        Conclusion 

        The latest updates in SAP Signavio Process Manager and Journey Modeler bring enhanced efficiency, greater control, and streamlined workflows to your decision modeling, process management, and journey optimization efforts. From the intuitive DMN TestLab UX to the seamless synchronization between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM, these advancements are designed to empower your teams with the tools they need for continuous improvement. Whether you’re simplifying report generation or fast-tracking journey modeling, these updates provide you with the agility and intelligence to drive your enterprise’s success. 

         

        Stay tuned for more exciting updates and innovations from SAP Signavio.
